assertions.assertion: The response explains that context.Fail() is a hard veto that prevents any other handler from satisfying the requirement
assertions.rationale: The response states 'When you call context.Fail(), it acts as a hard veto — it tells the ASP.NET Core authorization system that authorization must be denied, and no other handler can override this.'

assertions.assertion: The fix removes the context.Fail() call
assertions.rationale: The corrected code removes the context.Fail() call entirely, showing only the Succeed() path.

assertions.assertion: The corrected handler only calls context.Succeed(requirement) when the condition is met
assertions.rationale: The corrected code shows the handler only calling context.Succeed(requirement) inside the if block when the scope claim is present, and simply returning otherwise.

assertions.assertion: The response mentions that not calling Succeed() means 'no opinion' and allows other handlers to still satisfy the requirement
assertions.rationale: The response states 'When your handler doesn't call context.Succeed(), it essentially has "no opinion" on the requirement. This allows other handlers to still evaluate and potentially satisfy the requirement.'
